title: 控方证人
summary:
During a legal consultation, Leonard Wall maintains his innocence regarding the death of Miss Emily French, despite being in a serious financial crisis. Wall explains that he first met Miss French on Oxford Street after performing a simple act of kindness by helping her recover dropped packages. Later that evening, he encountered her at a party hosted by his friend, George Harvey. Wall claims he only learned of her wealth and eccentric lifestyle, including her residence in Crikwood and her eight cats, through Harvey.

Wall admits to visiting Miss French frequently, explaining that his motivation was not financial but rather a psychological response to her affection and his own indecisive nature. He states that after several visits, he grew fond of her and began managing her business affairs because she claimed to be inexperienced with investments. His lawyer, Mr. Mehn, challenges this by noting that Miss French’s maid, Janet McKenzie, and her bank manager described her as a capable businesswoman. However, Mehn privately considers the possibility that Miss French feigned incompetence to flatter Wall.

When Mehn asks if Wall ever embezzled funds to alleviate his financial troubles, Wall emphatically denies any underhanded dealings. Wall argues that his strongest defense is a lack of motive, asserting that if he had courted her for money, her death would have thwarted his goals. Mehn then reveals that Miss French left a will naming Wall as her primary beneficiary.
